source_evidence: Eq. (4): [A_1]_{p,q} = 1/true if nodes p,q share an element, 0/false otherwise or if p=q, resulting in a symmetric A_1 (bi-directional). 'Two nodes are neighbors and share level 1 connectivity if they belong to the same element in the base mesh.'
purpose: Builds the level-1 nodal connectivity (adjacency) matrix from the base mesh element connectivities, defining which node pairs are direct neighbors for ground structure member generation.
assumptions: Only nodal connectivity is used; no edge, facet, or volume information is needed
All nodes within an element are connected regardless (elements must be convex polytopes)
Diagonal entries are zero (no self connectivity)
formula_or_algorithm: [A_1]_{p,q} = 1 if nodes p and q belong to a common base-mesh element and p != q; 0 otherwise. Iterate over all elements; for every pair of nodes in an element set the corresponding symmetric entries to true.
validation_opportunities: unit test on a single hexahedron/wedge mesh (Fig. 2)

reuse_risk.explanation: Simple, standard adjacency-matrix construction from element connectivity; sparse and well-defined.

missing_information: Storage format (dense vs sparse) chosen in implementation
